name: HMS Whitaker (K 580)
events: My father acting PO Alan Carruthers, was the sole surviving senior person left after the loss of the bow, he was responsible for saving the ship and was awarded the BEM (Military) for this action. (1)
3 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HMS Whiteaker (Lt. G.P.W. Edwards, RN) and HMS Rupert (Lt. P.C.S. Black, RN).
Also practice attacks were made on these two frigates. (2)
26 Oct 1944 HMS Unrivalled (Lt. D.S. Brown, RNVR) conducted exercises off Larne with ships from the 1st Escort Group.

The 1st Escort Group was made up of the frigates HMS Affleck (Cdr.(Retd.) C. Gwinner, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN), HMS Bentley (Lt.Cdr. E.G. May, DSC, RN), HMS Capel (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN), HMS Garlies ( T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.M.M. Stamp, RNVR) and HMS Gore (Lt. J.V. Reeves-Brown, DSC, RN) and HMS Whiteaker (Lt. G.P.W. Edwards, RN). (3)
27 Oct 1944 HMS Unrivalled (Lt. D.S. Brown, RNVR) conducted exercises off Larne with ships from the 1st Escort Group.
